board.title: From Relational to Distributed
board.author: Julius Boateng
board.description: From early relational models to large-scale distributed systems, many foundational ideas about storing data remain the same.

clues.question: Query language developed during System R database project; not strictly relational, allows duplicates, nulls, and ordered results
Google's 2006 distributed wide-column storage system that influenced modern NoSQL databases
Database company inspired by early relational model research; first to market a commercial relational database in 1979
In-memory cache for web apps; influenced Redis and large-scale web caching
Computing giant behind System R database project that proved relational model practical
Google's 2004 framework that popularized large-scale distributed data processing; filters, sorts, then aggregates results
Open-source data processing framework inspired by Google's 2004 MapReduce and 2006 Bigtable papers
Creator of Postgres at UC Berkeley; Turing Award winner
Horizontal database scaling strategy that partitions data across servers; named after broken pottery fragments
Distributed systems theorem proposed by Eric Brewer stating only two of three can be guaranteed: consistency, availability, partition tolerance
Inventor of the relational model; Turing Award winner; Boyce-___ normal form
